methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Heven AeroTech is a Series B-stage company in Defense & Security seeking valuation guidance against a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the company's last post-money valuation of $1.0B, implying $1.9B. Peer-Median analysis benchmarks against five same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations spanning $1.0B to $1.5B; the median of this set is $1.4B. The peer range brackets the target's current $1.0B+ valuation, with Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at the high end ($1.5B each) and Castelion and Defense Unicorns at the low end ($1.0B each).

Triangulating both methods yields a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The $400M spread between methods reflects uncertainty in stage-progression assumptions versus peer comparability. Key sensitivities include peer selection (concentration in the $1.4B–$1.5B band) and the applicability of the 1.9x multiplier to this company's specific growth trajectory and market conditions within Defense & Security.
